message:
fix(serve/test): align TestParseJWT variants with new 5-value parseJWT signature

parseJWT was widened from a 4-value return tuple
`(header, claims, signingInput, signature, err)` — actually the
old shape was `(header, claims, signingInput, err)` without the
signature — to a 5-value return tuple
`(header, claims, signingInput, signature, err)` so the RS256
verification path can get the raw signature bytes without
re-parsing the token.  The core change lives in the serve/
package's parseJWT implementation; this commit just catches the
test harness up so `go test ./...` passes again.

internal/serve/server_test.go

Every test site that bound parseJWT's return through a
`_, _, _, err := parseJWT(...)` pattern is updated to
`_, _, _, _, err := parseJWT(...)`.  Affected tests include
the four TestParseJWT subtests (invalid format, only two parts,
invalid base64 header, malformed claims) plus the standalone
TestParseJWT_InvalidFormat regression guard.

All of these tests are error-path coverage that only care about
the error return, so the new unused-signature return binding is
just an extra `_` and the assertions are unchanged.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
